clue: These two outcast individuals were unexpectedly positioned on Jesus's right and left when he was elevated on the cross.
scriptureQuote: With Him they also crucified two robbers, one on His right and the other on His left.

clue: These two rulers, representing religious and political authorities, collaborated to sentence Jesus to death.
scriptureQuote: Then they led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Praetorium... Pilate then went out to them and said, 'What accusation do you bring against this Man?'

clue: These religious authorities accused Jesus of blasphemy when he claimed authority to forgive the paralytic's sins.
scriptureQuote: And some of the scribes were sitting there and reasoning in their hearts, “Why does this Man speak blasphemies like this? Who can forgive sins but God alone?”

clue: This foundational ancestor of humanity is who Jesus claims the Sabbath was originally instituted for in Mark 2.
scriptureQuote: And He said to them, “The Sabbath was made for man, and not man for the Sabbath.

clue: This pristine garden sanctuary is what the disciples' grain-picking on the Sabbath is structurally linked to.
scriptureQuote: The Lord God planted a garden eastward in Eden, and there He put the man whom He had formed. And out of the ground the Lord God made every tree grow that is pleasant to the sight and good for food. The tree of life was also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.

clue: This holy city is where Jesus journeyed to confront the beastly powers and offer his life on the cross.
scriptureQuote: Now they were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was going before them; and they were amazed. And as they followed they were afraid.
